Alexander M. Drummond papers
Drummond, A. M. (Alexander Magnus), 1884-1956.
Van Loon, Hendrik Willem, 1882-1944.
Shaw, Bernard, 1856-1950.
Hammerstein, Oscar, II, 1895-1960.
O\'Neill, Eugene, 1888-1953.
14-24-435
19.2 cubic feet. 19.2 cubic feet.
1917-1956. Correspondence, news clippings, scripts, programs, playbills, lecture notes, theater notes, photographs, recordings and other memorabilia. Correspondence, news clippings, scripts, programs, playbills, lecture notes, theater notes, photographs, recordings and other memorabilia relating to various aspects of the field of Speech and Drama. Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Date completed:
December 1966September 2010
EAD encoding:
Evan Earle, September 2010
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, September 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Speech and Drama, Cornell University.
Organized the Cornell Dramatic Club and was its Director from 1912-1947. Wrote original plays and a monograph on play production.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Biographical
Series II. Correspondence
Series III. Plays and Writings
Series IV. Teaching and Professorship
Series V. Photographs and Glass Slides
Series VI. Clippings
Series VII. Scripts
Series VIII. Programs
Series IX. Oversized Items
Series X. Audio
Series XI. Miscellaneous
RELATED MATERIALS
Includes collection #14-24-1143.
SEPARATED MATERIAL
Kingsley, Sidney. Men in White. Signed by the author.
The Playground. v.13, no.2,4,9 (1919)
The New Yorker. October 2, 1954.
Hatch, Emily Gilchriest, 1897-. On the road to market. Trivandrum : Keston House, 1938.
The Public Speaking Review. Public Speaking Conference of New England and the North Atlantic States.
The Speech Teacher. v.5, no.3 (September 1956).
World premieres : monthly bulletin of the International Theatre Institute. International Theatre Institute. v.1, no.3-6 (1949-1950).
General
Selected correspondence available on microfilm.
Cite As:
Alexander M. Drummond papers, #14-24-435. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, news clippings, scripts, programs, playbills, lecture notes, theater notes, photographs, recordings and other memorabilia relating to various aspects of the field of Speech and Drama.
Scope and Contents
Includes charcoal drawing of Professor Drummond by Christian Midjo.
Scope and Contents
Includes correspondence from Eugene O'Neill, Oscar Hammerstein II, George Bernard Shaw and Hendrik Van Loon.
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, etc. with the Speech Association of America relating to graduate research, 1947.
Scope and Contents
Text of speeches given by Dean Paul M. O'Leary, President Deane W. Malott, Professor Herbert A. Wichelns and Professor Drummond at a testimonial dinner for Drummond, May 14, 1952.
Theater programs. Playbills. Scripts (documents) Theatrical publishing. Theater reviews. College theater -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Drama -- Study and teaching. Cornell University. Department of Speech and Drama Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University. Cornell Dramatic Club
